description:
You are a Linux systems engineer preparing the next minor release of the in-house “sys-hardener” project that lives in:

/home/user/sys-hardener

The repository already contains:

1. /home/user/sys-hardener/config.yaml  
   - one of the keys is named version and is currently set to 1.3.4

2. /home/user/sys-hardener/CHANGELOG.md  
   - it follows “Keep a Changelog” style headings (`## [x.y.z] - YYYY-MM-DD`)
   - the most recent entry is for version 1.3.3

Your goal  
A. Perform a semantic **minor** version bump from 1.3.4 to 1.4.0 in exactly two places:  
   1. The version field inside config.yaml  
   2. The top of CHANGELOG.md

B. Prepend a brand-new changelog section that must be the very first lines of CHANGELOG.md.  
   Strict format (the automated test will read this literally, including spacing and punctuation):

   Line 1: `## [1.4.0] - 2023-08-15`  
   Line 2: *(blank)*  
   Line 3: `### Added`  
   Line 4: `- Default SSH hardening rules for key-only authentication.`  

C. Leave all older changelog sections unmodified and placed **after** the new block.

Verification hints  
• The test will parse config.yaml, expecting the YAML scalar `version` to equal the literal string `1.4.0`.  
• It will read the first four non-empty lines of CHANGELOG.md and compare them exactly to the four lines given above (including the two leading hash symbols, the single space after them, brackets, hyphen spacing, headline capitalization, and final period).  
• No other file should be created, modified, or deleted.

Make sure to:

1. Navigate to the correct directory before editing.  
2. Use tools of your choice (sed, printf >>, editors, etc.) but keep the required output format exact.  
3. Save all changes to disk so the test can read them.

The entire task can be completed with two or three shell commands.
truth:
Initial state before the agent runs:

File: /home/user/sys-hardener/config.yaml  
Contents:  
version: 1.3.4  
hardening:  
  ssh:  
    permit_root_login: "no"  
    password_authentication: "no"  

File: /home/user/sys-hardener/CHANGELOG.md  
Contents:  
## [1.3.3] - 2023-07-01  
### Fixed  
- Corrected sysctl typo that prevented IPv6 RA filtering.  

## [1.3.2] - 2023-06-10  
### Security  
- Updated default TLS cipher suite list.

Expected final state after the agent runs:

/home/user/sys-hardener/config.yaml  
Contents (exact):  
version: 1.4.0  
hardening:  
  ssh:  
    permit_root_login: "no"  
    password_authentication: "no"  

/home/user/sys-hardener/CHANGELOG.md  
Contents must begin with exactly these four lines followed by a blank line and then the old content:

## [1.4.0] - 2023-08-15

### Added
- Default SSH hardening rules for key-only authentication.

## [1.3.3] - 2023-07-01  
### Fixed  
- Corrected sysctl typo that prevented IPv6 RA filtering.  

## [1.3.2] - 2023-06-10  
### Security  
- Updated default TLS cipher suite list.

(Everything after the new 1.4.0 section is byte-for-byte identical to the original file.)

The automated test will fail if:
• config.yaml’s version is not “1.4.0” or any other line is altered accidentally;  
• the first four non-empty lines of CHANGELOG.md deviate in any character (including date);  
• older sections are missing or modified;  
• any extra files appear inside /home/user/sys-hardener.

Both files are owned by user “user” and writable without elevated privileges.
name: task_000000_fffbc984
